sections.heading: Zero conditional
sections.body: Structure: If + present simple, present simple. Used for universal truths, scientific facts, and things that always happen as a consequence of something else. 'If' can be replaced with 'when' without changing meaning.

sections.heading: First conditional
sections.body: Structure: If + present simple, will + infinitive. Used for real, likely situations in the present or future. The 'if' clause sets up a realistic condition; the main clause states its probable result.

sections.heading: Other modals in first conditional
sections.body: The main clause doesn't have to use 'will'. You can use: might (less certain), can (ability), should (advice), imperative: 'If you're hungry, help yourself.'

examples.english: If you heat ice, it melts.
If it rains tomorrow, I'll stay home.
If you see her, tell her I called.
If you're tired, you might want to rest.

items: If you ___ (mix) red and blue, you ___ (get) purple.
If she ___ (study) harder, she ___ (pass) the exam.

quiz.question: Which conditional is used for real, likely future situations?
quiz.explanation: First conditional (if + present simple, will + infinitive) describes real, probable future situations.
